fields.description:
# Persist ticket digest as a REVIEW_RESULT-style sidecar (shared cache)

## Why
The digest is a billable LLM artifact we never want to recompute (~$1.90 to enrich the whole 793-ticket store — see epic experiment comment). Storing it in the SHARED store (auto-pushed) makes one client's enrichment a cache-hit for everyone. rebar's exact precedent is `REVIEW_RESULT`: a reducer-IGNORED, preserved-and-ignored, per-ticket LLM-derived sidecar with a schema sentinel + a `prune` retention. We mirror it.

## What — payload schema (fully specified)
A `TICKET_DIGEST` sidecar event. Its `data` payload:
`{"schema": "ticket_digest_v1", "digest": {problem_keywords, component_or_area, key_entities, propositions}, "content_hash": "<hex>", "model": "<model-id>", "digest_hash_version": <int>}`.
The `schema` sentinel `"ticket_digest_v1"` guards reads (mirrors REVIEW_RESULT keying on `payload['schema'] == 'plan_review_result_v1'`) so a foreign/older payload is skipped, not mis-parsed. `content_hash`, `model`, `digest_hash_version` are the three freshness tags (named fields in the payload).

## Content hash — REUSE the canonical normalizer (promoted to public)
`content_hash = sha256(ticket_text(state))` where `ticket_text` is the EXISTING canonical join
in `src/rebar/_engine_support/gates.py` (title + description + comment bodies, `"\n".join`),
currently the module-PRIVATE `_ticket_text`. Because `digest_sidecar.py` lives in a different
package subtree (`rebar.llm.overlap`), importing a single-underscore private across that
boundary is a layering violation — so this story PROMOTES it to a public `ticket_text()` in
`gates.py` (keeping `_ticket_text = ticket_text` as a back-compat alias for existing in-module
callers) and both this story and S5 (enrich) import the public name. We do NOT define a new
normalization — divergence would silently mis-mark freshness. `DIGEST_HASH_VERSION` (module
constant in the digest sidecar) is bumped only if the hashed field-set ever changes,
invalidating all cached digests deterministically. (If S5/`ee3d`, lower in the stack, has
already promoted `ticket_text`, this story simply imports it and does not re-promote.)

## Freshness (computed on read, fail-closed)
The digest is CURRENT iff ALL of: stored `content_hash` == `sha256(ticket_text(current))`,
stored `model` == active model, stored `digest_hash_version` == current constant. Any mismatch
→ stale. If current state can't be read (partial read) → NOT-current (fail-closed; never
falsely fresh).

## Scope (exact edit targets — every registry a new event type touches)
- `src/rebar/_engine_support/gates.py`: promote `_ticket_text` -> public `ticket_text()` (keep `_ticket_text = ticket_text` back-compat alias) so cross-package callers (`overlap.digest_sidecar`, S5's `enrich`) import a public name. (No-op if S5 already promoted it.)
- `src/rebar/_engine_support/reads_cli.py`: the `_cmd_show` freshness render (detailed below).
- `src/rebar/_store/event_append.py`: add `"TICKET_DIGEST"` to the `EVENT_TYPES` write allow-list AND its enumerated string in the StoreError message (lines ~97-103).
- `src/rebar/reducer/_version.py`: add `"TICKET_DIGEST"` to `_NON_REPLAY_KNOWN_TYPES` (NOT `KNOWN_EVENT_TYPES`) — preserved-and-ignored, never compacted into SNAPSHOT, no fsck WARN (mirrors REVIEW_RESULT).
- New `src/rebar/llm/overlap/__init__.py` (the package) + `src/rebar/llm/overlap/digest_sidecar.py` with `emit(digest, ticket_id)` (appends + calls `prune`), `prune(ticket_id, keep=1)`, `latest_ticket_digest(ticket_id)` (guards on the `ticket_digest_v1` sentinel) — all modeled on `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/sidecar.py`.
- `show` freshness render (CLI-show-ONLY — must NOT touch the library state shape):
  the parity invariant `test_show_list_search_share_one_shape`
  (`tests/interfaces/store/test_reducer_single_source.py:59`) asserts the **library** returns
  are identical — `set(rebar.show_ticket(id)) == set(list_tickets element) == set(search element)`
  and `show == lst == srch`. So `show_state`/`show_ticket` and `reducer/_present.py::public_state`
  (the SHARED key-stripping filter used by show/list/search/ready/MCP — NOT a show-only
  renderer) must be left untouched. The freshness flag ("present-fresh"|"present-stale"|"absent")
  is therefore added ONLY in the CLI show arm `src/rebar/_engine_support/reads_cli.py::_cmd_show`
  (~line 144-171): after `state = show_state(...)`, `_cmd_show` computes freshness via
  `overlap.digest_sidecar.latest_ticket_digest(id)` + the freshness check and injects
  `digest_freshness` into the dict it prints (`json.dumps(state, ...)` and the `to_llm(state)`
  `--output llm` arm) — a CLI-presentation-only field that never enters the library
  `show_ticket`/`list`/`search` returns, so the parity invariant holds. No new field in
  `ticket_state.schema.json`; the digest stays a sidecar (like REVIEW_RESULT).
  `test_cmd_show_renders_freshness` asserts the CLI `show` stdout carries the flag;
  `test_library_shape_unchanged` asserts `show_ticket`/`list`/`search` still share one shape
  with a digest present (parity invariant held).
- Prune invocation: `emit` calls `prune(keep=1)` on every write — EXACTLY the `REVIEW_RESULT` sidecar pattern, nothing more. There is NO special archive/delete lazy-prune (that novel behavior is dropped: it has no `REVIEW_RESULT` prior art and `show_state` does not cleanly signal "gone" for archived tickets). An archived/deleted ticket's digest simply lingers as a harmless sidecar, bounded to ONE record per ticket by `keep=1`, and is never read into any hot path (like a stale REVIEW_RESULT). `latest_ticket_digest` does no existence check on the ticket.

## Dependencies
S1 (indecisive-callous-armyworm) — produces the `ticket_digest` payload. (The drain that decides to re-enrich lives in S4; this story owns only WRITE + READ + freshness of the sidecar, not the skip decision.)

## Testing
- `tests/unit/test_ticket_digest_store.py`: `test_roundtrip`; `test_freshness_all_triggers` (stale on content-hash OR model OR digest_hash_version change); `test_hash_parity` (this story's `content_hash` recomputed over the same ticket state via `ticket_text` is byte-stable — same input → same hash — so freshness never mis-marks); `test_fail_closed` (unreadable → not-current); `test_latest_wins` (two appends, latest-by-HLC); `test_older_binary_ignores` (fsck no WARN); `test_cmd_show_renders_freshness` (CLI `show` stdout carries the flag); `test_library_shape_unchanged` (show_ticket/list/search still share one shape with a digest present); `test_prune_keep1` (a second `emit` supersedes the first — exactly one `TICKET_DIGEST` retained per ticket).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `TICKET_DIGEST` write→`latest_ticket_digest` read roundtrip with the `ticket_digest_v1` sentinel guard; `pytest ...::test_roundtrip`.
- [ ] Freshness flips stale on ANY of content-hash / model / digest_hash_version mismatch; `::test_freshness_all_triggers`. content_hash uses `_ticket_text` (parity with S1); `::test_hash_parity`.
- [ ] `TICKET_DIGEST` in `EVENT_TYPES` + `_NON_REPLAY_KNOWN_TYPES` (not `KNOWN_EVENT_TYPES`); older binary preserves-and-ignores, no fsck WARN; `::test_older_binary_ignores`.
- [ ] Unreadable current state → NOT-current (fail-closed); `::test_fail_closed`.
- [ ] The CLI `show` stdout renders digest presence + freshness (not raw text) via `_cmd_show`; `::test_cmd_show_renders_freshness`. The library `show_ticket`/`list`/`search` returns still share one shape with a digest present (parity invariant `test_show_list_search_share_one_shape` untouched); `::test_library_shape_unchanged`. `emit` keeps exactly one `TICKET_DIGEST` per ticket (`prune(keep=1)`, REVIEW_RESULT pattern; no archive/delete special-casing); `::test_prune_keep1`.
